metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: Neon Dynasty is one of the slowest recent Limited formats, emphasizing card advantage and value over aggressive strategies. The set features 10 dual-color archetypes with flexible deckbuilding options.
color_ranking: All five colors are balanced — pair-driven format.

color_notes.{W} White: Strong removal (Intercessor's Arrest, Repel the Vile) and value creatures. Pairs well with blue for vehicles and black for control.
color_notes.{U} Blue: Excellent ninjutsu enablers and tempo creatures. Moon-Circuit Hacker and Moonsnare Specialist drive the archetype.
color_notes.{B} Black: Rich in sagas and sacrifice synergies. Okiba Reckoner Raid is a premier common across multiple archetypes.
color_notes.{R} Red: Efficient removal and artifact synergies. Experimental Synthesizer generates card advantage in dedicated decks.
color_notes.{G} Green: Best color for mana fixing and enchantment density. Tales of Master Seshiro and Jukai Preserver anchor strategies.

key_gameplay_notes: Slow format — card advantage matters more than tempo.
Prioritize hard removal over soft removal given format speed.
Evaluate sagas as multi-turn value rather than immediate board impact.
Ninjutsu costs decrease significantly through alternate casting, enabling multiple activations per turn.
10 common dual lands provide baseline fixing.

mechanics.name: Reconfigure
mechanics.summary: Artifact creatures function as both creatures and equipment, providing flexibility without overpaying.
mechanics.performance: Universal utility; slots into any deck.

mechanics.name: Modified
mechanics.summary: Creatures with enchantments, equipment, or counters trigger payoffs.

mechanics.name: Sagas
mechanics.summary: Double-faced cards that transform into creatures at Chapter III. Play as value engines in midrange decks.
mechanics.performance: Strong in midrange archetypes — multi-turn value.

key_examples.name: Tales of Master Seshiro
key_examples.type_line: Enchantment — Saga // Enchantment Creature — Snake Warrior

key_examples.name: Life of Toshiro Umezawa
key_examples.type_line: Enchantment — Saga // Enchantment Creature — Human Samurai

key_examples.name: Michiko's Reign of Truth
key_examples.type_line: Enchantment — Saga // Enchantment Creature — Human Noble

mechanics.name: Ninjutsu
mechanics.summary: Return an unblocked attacker to hand and put the ninja onto the battlefield tapped and attacking. Enables tempo strategies with evasive enablers.

mechanics.name: Channel
mechanics.summary: Optional alternate effects activated by discarding the card. Provides flexibility without deckbuilding constraints.
mechanics.performance: Adds versatility; useful in graveyard decks.

archetypes.name: Azorius Vehicles
archetypes.strategy: Plays oversized vehicles with Pilot token generators. Signpost focuses on crew efficiency.

archetypes.name: Dimir Ninjas
archetypes.strategy: Tempo strategy assembling cheap creatures with ninja payoffs. Dominates through evasion and value.

archetypes.name: Rakdos Sacrifice
archetypes.strategy: Rewards creature sacrifice. Aggressive early game transitioning to value.

archetypes.name: Gruul Modified
archetypes.strategy: Stacks enchantments, equipment, and counters on creatures for payoff triggers.

archetypes.name: Orzhov Balance
archetypes.strategy: Control-oriented with dual-type payoffs (artifacts AND enchantments).

archetypes.name: Izzet Artifacts
archetypes.strategy: Artifact-focused with Patchwork Automaton and Dragonspark Reactor generating value from artifact density.

archetypes.name: Golgari Graveyard
archetypes.strategy: Leverages channel mechanics and reanimation for late-game advantage.

archetypes.name: Boros Samurai
archetypes.strategy: Aggressive with pseudo-exalted abilities and first-strike payoffs.

archetypes.name: Simic Ramp
archetypes.strategy: Mana acceleration into large threats, utilizing channel creatures for graveyard recursion value.

removal_priority.note: Prioritize hard removal over soft removal given the format's slow speed and high-value creatures.

general_tips: Card advantage proves essential in extended games.
Evaluate sagas as multi-turn value, not immediate board impact.
Ninjutsu costs decrease through alternate casting — plan for multiple activations per turn.
Green provides the best fixing via Grafted Growth and Greater Tanuki.
Hard removal beats soft removal in this slow format.
